You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You self-scope from the recommendation, roadmap, and opened inbox below.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level: experiment, change the strategy entirely, try alternative formulations, edit blueprint material when proving teaches you something, and — when a task calls for it — work across the several projects it spans, or create and merge projects. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the projects in scope and the shared `references/`, don't leave stray directories or scratch files behind. The Ground agent and its review subagents run after you to reconcile, check math/blueprint alignment, and tidy — so prefer making progress over being cautious.

How to work:
- Read the relevant blueprint node and proof sketch before touching Lean.
- Run the project's build/check command (or the narrowest useful Lean check) and repair failures you introduce. Don't bury obligations under new `sorry`s unless a hint allows it.
- Use the inbox to comment, close, create, and message other projects with `--author horizon` (see the horizon-inbox skill). ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, and reports in Markdown — short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. Give every item a short title and a non-empty description. Comment at each key advance, and add a concise closing comment (runs, LOC, files) before you `complete` an item. Treat `[persistent]` items as standing rules; close `[temporary]` ones once consumed.
- When your work completes or blocks a task/roadmap milestone, leave a concise task or roadmap comment with the decisive checks, changed files, and remaining caveats. If a task has linked `roadmap_refs`, keep your report/comment explicit about the status each linked roadmap item should have so Ground or the human can keep the roadmap consistent.
- Record durable dead ends as memory: `horizon inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --author horizon --body "..."`, so later sessions don't repeat them.
- Commit your work yourself as you finish coherent pieces — `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>` (see the horizon-commit skill). Small semantic commits (a lemma closed, a file completed) make the history and the dashboard's change view legible; don't leave one undifferentiated blob for the end-of-session sweep. Other runs may commit concurrently to the same ledger — that's fine; commit only the files you changed.
- You may delegate to subagents when it helps (see the Subagents section): split a wide search across parallel read-only subagents, hand mechanical checks to a cheaper-model subagent, or pull references. You are free to decide whether they fit the task.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC (axiom-clean). The AJC in-tree copy is now fully v4.31-clean (T8, commit `eed5383`); the standalone `Cech-Cohomology` subproject still carries the 16 migration-interim `sorry`.
- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). Two open leaves: `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` (Stacks 02KG, termwise affine BC via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact). See task **T2**.

$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.

- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).

- ALB.ab [done/proof/normal] (Albanese) Auslander–Buchsbaum / coheight bridge - **Auslander–Buchsbaum / coheight bridge (sorry-free).** `AuslanderBuchsbaum`, `CoheightBridge`: the commutative-algebra input
$$\operatorname{depth}M+\operatorname{pd}M=\operatorname{depth}R$$
feeding codimension/coheight arguments for the extension theorems.

- ALB.codim1 [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension - **Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension.** A rational map from a smooth variety to an abelian variety extends off a locus of codimension $\ge 2$:
$$X\dashrightarrow A\ \Longrightarrow\ X\setminus Z\to A,\ \operatorname{codim}Z\ge 2.$$
Milne 3.1 (codim-$\ge2$ indeterminacy, valuative) and the empty-locus extension theorem are proved; `Thm32RationalMapExtension` is sorry-free. $$\forall\,\phi:(C,p)\to A,\ \exists!\ \bar{\phi}:\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\to A\ \text{with}\ \phi=\bar\phi\circ\mathrm{aj}.$$

for separated quasi-compact $f$ and a finite affine cover — unconditional (no enough-injectives appeal). Deliverable merged into AJC.

Dead code note: `stalkMap_flat_of_smooth` (Stage 1) and `exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth` (Stage 3) in `CodimOneExtension.lean` now have NO Lean consumers (the new stalk proof bypasses them); blueprint-pinned leaves — Ground may prune or keep.
- **leandag audit convention: the DAG engine registers only STATEMENT-level `\uses`; proof-level `\uses` are ignored.**

- Consequence: a blueprint node whose only dependencies are declared inside its proof block shows as **isolated** in `horizon leandag`, even though the math dependency is real and the proof is complete. This is expected, not a blueprint defect.
- Observed run 0006 (Albanese): 297 nodes, **178 isolated**, but **0 dangling** and 287 proved — the high isolated count is entirely this artifact.
- **The health metric to trust is `dangling` (should be 0) and `proved`, NOT `isolated`.** Do not chase isolated nodes as missing edges; if you want them linked in the DAG, lift the relevant `\uses` from the proof block up to the statement line.
- Same engine limitation was noted by the T6 Horizon session; recorded here so future Ground audits don't misread it.
- **IdentityComponent `isSubgroupHomomorphism` + `baseChangeIso` CLOSED, axiom-clean (run 0005 session 0011, T5).** File sorries 8 → 6; all 6 remaining are blocked (QC∧GeomIrred conjunct needs EGA IV₂ 4.6.1-type input; 5 Pic⁰ decls FGA-tainted). Recipes:

- **Subgroup object via Yoneda**: `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Subgroup (T ⟶ G)` presheaf (`{f | Set.range ⇑f.left ⊆ carrier}`) beats hand-rolling MonObj/GrpObj diagram axioms. Closure under mul: factor `f*g = lift f' g' ≫ ((ι⊗ₘι) ≫ μ)` via `lift_map`, bound range by core lemma `range ((ι⊗ₘι)≫μ).left ⊆ carrier` (connected product through e). `Hom.group` is a **scoped instance**: `open scoped MonObj`.
- **`G⁰ ×ₖ G⁰` connected**: Mathlib `[GeometricallyConnected g] [UniversallyOpen g] [ConnectedSpace X] : ConnectedSpace (pullback f g)` + `(R ⊗ S).left = pullback R.hom S.hom` (rfl). UniversallyOpen-to-Spec-field needs `haveI : Subsingleton ↥(Spec (.of k)) := inferInstanceAs (Subsingleton (PrimeSpectrum k))` (sometimes ALSO at the defeq-disguised type `↥(𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))).left`).
- **grpObjMkPullbackSnd unit compat** (`fst(e_{G_K}) = e_G`): `Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd_one : one = ε ≫ (Over.pullback g).map one` IS rw-able against a `letI`-bound instance (must be `letI`, not `haveI` — GrpObj is data). But **morphism-level rw/assoc in the resulting goal FAILS**: `(Over.pullback φ).obj X` vs `pullback X.hom φ` index mismatch is opaque at instances transparency and `respectTransparency false` does NOT rescue rw or post-rw rfl. Working pattern: **point level** — calc chain of `rfl` steps redistributing `⇑(_ ≫ _).base` applications, `congrArg` for every rewrite (incl. `Subsingleton.elim`), `rw` only inside standalone well-typed subterms (e.g. `pullback.lift_fst` on an explicitly spelled lift, with the lift's w-proof given concretely — proof irrelevance makes the rfl steps match).
- **Post-rw auto-rfl failures** on ⇑-coercion goals (identical-looking sides left unsolved): replace `by rw [h]` with term-mode `congrArg (fun m => …) h`.
- **baseChangeIso iso assembly**: `IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq (carrier G_K).ι (pullback.snd ι fst) ≪≫ pullbackRightPullbackFstIso G.hom φ ι`; carrier identification `fst⁻¹(carrier G) = carrier G_K` by clopen (⊇) + preconnected-range (⊆) around `IsOpenImmersion.range_pullbackSnd`.
- `SubProjects/Picard-IdentityComponent` mirror now FURTHER behind AJC (needs re-sync or retirement).
- **Recipe: ValuativeCommSq for rational-map extension (Milne 3.1 codim-≥2 CLOSED); over-k̄ hypothesis is required.**

`indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (Albanese `CodimOneExtension.lean`) is proved axiom-clean — Mathlib v4.31 has ALL the plumbing; do not hand-roll rational-map/valuative machinery. Reusable recipe:

- **Pipeline**: `f.fromFunctionField : Spec K(X) ⟶ Y` + Mathlib `IsFractionRing (stalk z) K(X)` (integral schemes) + DVR stalk ⇒ `ValuativeCommSq Y.hom`; extract the lift from `IsProper.eq_valuativeCriterion` (`rw at hP; exact hP.1.1.1`); spread out with `PartialMap.ofFromSpecStalk X.hom Y.hom L hfacr` (`[X.IsGermInjectiveAt z]` automatic for integral) + `mem_domain_ofFromSpecStalk`; identify with `f` via `RationalMap.eq_of_fromFunctionField_eq` + a small glue lemma `fromFunctionField = Spec.map (stalkSpecializes gen⤳z) ≫ fromSpecStalkOfMem` (prove by `cancel_mono g.domain.ι` + `Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_ι` + `SpecMap_stalkSpecializes_fromSpecStalk`).
- `CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap (stalk z) functionField) = stalkSpecializes ((genericPoint_spec X).specializes trivial)` is **rfl** (`stalkFunctionFieldAlgebra` def) — exploit by defeq (`exact`), do NOT `rw` (higher-order pattern fails).
- **Structure-literal trap**: inside a `ValuativeCommSq { ... }` literal the stalk term gets unfolded past `Scheme.presheaf`, so keyed instances (`IsDomain (stalk z)`, `Field functionField`, `IsFractionRing`, `stalkFunctionFieldAlgebra`) STOP firing. Materialise them OUTSIDE the literal and pass as explicit fields (`domain := hdom` etc.).
- **Field-poisoning trap**: bind those facts with plain `have`/`let`, NEVER `haveI/letI` — an opaque local `Field functionField` fvar shadows the CommRing path and makes `Algebra (stalk z) K(X)` unsynthesizable. Also needs `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` BEFORE the docstring (IsField.toField / CommRingCat.of-eta walls).
- **Over-k̄ is mathematically necessary**: the valuative square's base compatibility needs `hf : f.compHom Y.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap` (Milne's maps are k̄-maps). The whole Thm-3.2 chain now threads this hypothesis; future consumers must supply it (for maps built from k̄-morphisms it follows via `Hom.toRationalMap`/`compHom_toRationalMap`). Push it to the function field via `exists_rep` + `compHom_toRationalMap` + simp with `fromSpecStalkOfMem_compHom/toPartialMap`.
- Coheight conventions: scheme points carry `specializationPreorder` (Mathlib instance, `x ≤ y ↔ y ⤳ x`); coheight 0 ⇔ `IsMax` (`Order.coheight_eq_zero`) ⇒ generic point via `Specializes.antisymm ...|>.eq`; `ENat.lt_two_iff` for the ≤1 split.
- Remaining CodimOneExtension gaps are NOT valuative: Stacks 0AVF (depth-≥2 H¹, Milne 3.1 Step 2) and Milne 3.3 difference-map machinery.
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- Recipe: pole-divisor purity WITHOUT normality/UFD of regular local rings (Albanese `PolePurity.lean`, axiom-clean) — reuse for any "unmixedness of principal ideals" need.

Mathlib v4.31 has NO regular⟹UFD (Auslander–Buchsbaum), NO Serre normality criterion, NO Krull-domain $R=\bigcap_{\mathrm{ht}=1}R_\mathfrak p$; `RingTheory/RegularLocalRing/` is only `Defs.lean`. Do not build those. The elementary substitute chain:

- **Swap lemma** (`mem_span_singleton_of_swap_pair`): domain, $u\ne 0$, $v$ regular mod $(u)$, $ux, vx \in (b)$ ⟹ $x \in (b)$ via $b(ut-vs)=0$. Replaces the determinant trick / integral-closedness entirely.
- **Regular pair in regular local of dim ≥ 2** (`IsRegularLocalRing.exists_swap_pair_of_two_le_ringKrullDim`): $u \in \mathfrak m\setminus\mathfrak m^2$ + project lemmas `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq` and `isDomain_of_regularLocal` (00NP, AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean — CONFIRMED axiom-clean); pick $v$ lifting a nonzero element of the quotient's maximal ideal.
- **Ass-prime height bound** (`Ideal.height_le_one_of_colon_span_singleton`): prime $Q=((b):y)$ with $A_Q$ regular ⟹ $\mathrm{ht}\,Q\le 1$; the colon persists to $\mathfrak m_{A_Q}$ (map ≤ colon via `Localization.AtPrime.map_eq_maximalIdeal`, colon proper ⟹ ≤ maximalIdeal).
- **Main thm over $R_\mathfrak p$** so the associated prime lands below $\mathfrak p$ (`exists_le_isAssociatedPrime_of_isNoetherianRing` + `isAssociatedPrime_iff`); transfer with the golden Mathlib trio: `IsLocalization.height_under` (heights under comap of localization), `IsLocalization.localizationLocalizationAtPrimeIsoLocalization` (double localization ≃ₐ), `IsRegularLocalRing.of_ringEquiv`.
- **Scheme wrapper traps**: state membership as `algebraMap (X.presheaf.stalk z) X.functionField).range` (instances `stalkFunctionFieldAlgebra`, `functionField_isScalarTower`, `functionField_isFractionRing_of_isAffineOpen` are all ready-made for integral X); point-from-prime via `hU.fromSpec` + `hU.isLocalization_stalk'` (takes the PrimeSpectrum point directly — no primeIdealOf round-trip); `Order.coheight z = 1` via CoheightBridge `ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` + `IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height`. Hypotheses of form `∀ q, IsRegularLocalRing (Localization.AtPrime q)` MUST use instance binder `∀ (q : Ideal R) [q.IsPrime], …` (plain `→` fails elaboration). `Submodule.mem_colon_singleton` gives `r • y ∈ …` — always explicit `rw [smul_eq_mul]`, never `simpa [smul_eq_mul] using` (fails). `IsLocalization.mk'_surjective` returns a beta-unreduced match — fix with `replace hcs : IsLocalization.mk' … = g := hcs`. Quotient-module annihilator bridge: `Algebra.smul_def` + `Ideal.Quotient.algebraMap_eq` (NOT `map_smul`/`Submodule.Quotient.mk_smul`).

Remaining Milne 3.3 gaps after this: substeps 1–3 (difference rational map + slicing + $\Phi^*\mathcal O_{G,e}$; needs function-field pullback for `Scheme.RationalMap`) and 4b (diagonal codim bound: `ringKrullDim_le_ringKrullDim_add_card` in Mathlib `KrullDimension/Regular.lean` is the Krull tool; diagonal ideal $n$-generation from smoothness still to build). For non-closed diagonal points, the slicing in substep 2 works at the GENERIC point of the fiber $\mathrm{pr}_1^{-1}(x) = \operatorname{Spec}\kappa(x)\times_{\bar k}X$ (irreducible by geometric irreducibility; its generic point maps to $\eta_X \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)$ under $\mathrm{pr}_2$) — this resolves the "Milne argues with closed points" worry.
- **Rescue-commit recipe when `horizon commit` fails (missing git identity / staging no-op).**

Seen run 0007 (session 0013-ground); root cause + human fix tracked in `I-0072`.

- **Symptom:** `horizon commit` exits with `failed:` / `Author identity unknown`, or silently stages nothing and reports "nothing to commit". The workspace + project ledgers had `user.name`/`user.email` unset everywhere (global too).
- **Fix step 1 (unblocks the end-of-session sweep too):** set repo-local identity on every ledger — `for r in .archon-horizon/vcs/*.git; do git --git-dir="$r" config user.name "Archon Horizon"; git --git-dir="$r" config user.email "archon-horizon@local"; done`.
- **Fix step 2 (if `horizon commit` still stages nothing):** commit manually into the **project** repo (that is where the `horizon[...]` deliverable history lives; the workspace repo only holds `integrate NNNN-session` commits). Paths are **relative to the project work-tree**, not the workspace root:
  - `G="git --git-dir=.archon-horizon/vcs/<Project>.git --work-tree=<project-path>"`
  - `$G add AlgebraicJacobian/...` then `$G commit --no-verify -m "role[<run> r<N> <task>](<Project>): <title>\n\n<body>\n\nCo-Authored-By: ..."`.
  - Subject format mirrors existing commits: `ground[0007 r0 T8](Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge): ...`. `Archon-*` trailers are only for the workspace repo, not needed here.
- Only manually commit **your own, verified** files (`.lean`/blueprint carry no secrets, so the bypassed secret-guard is moot); leave other tasks' in-flight edits for the sweep.

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action — never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, and `## Next`; add or rename sections if another shape is clearer. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this Horizon session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

data.content:
---
name: horizon-inbox
description: Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
---

The inbox is the durable channel between the human, the Ground agent, the
Horizon agent, and other projects. Act on it through the CLI; never edit inbox
files by hand. Prefer `--json` whenever reading command output for exact ids or
fields.

## Read

- `horizon inbox list` — read the inbox. Use `--json` when you need exact ids,
  labels, scope, author, audience, timestamps, or comments. Use the list filters
  to narrow without losing the overview: `--to horizon`, `--to ground`,
  `--to human`, `--project P`, repeated `--kind K`, repeated `--label L`,
  `--status open`, `--query TEXT`, `--limit N`, and `--comments N`.
- Labels are the release gate. Local inbox items default to `agent-ready`, which
  means they are visible to agents at run boundaries. Keep that default for
  machine-addressed work, and usually keep it for human-addressed notices too:
  it helps later agents see that the human has already been notified and avoids
  duplicate reports. Use `not-ready`, `rejected`, or no labels only when there is
  a concrete reason agents should not see or act on the item yet.
- Audience says who should read the item: empty/general, `horizon`, `ground`,
  `human`, or `project:<name>`. Scope/project says what the item is about.
- Horizon reads general items, items addressed to `horizon`, and items addressed
  to its project. It should ignore other projects' addressed items.
- Kinds: `hint` (normal guidance), `issue` (problem to fix/report),
  `protection` (standing constraint / soft freeze), `info` (a notice for the
  human — something you did or noticed they should know, e.g. a renamed project
  or an important change; purely informational, never affects what runs),
  `memory` (durable agent note or dead end).

## Act

Authorship is automatic: every item and comment you create through the CLI is
attributed to your role (`ground` or `horizon`) from the run environment — you do
**not** pass `--author` (a stray `--author blueprint-reviewer` is demoted to the
`agent` metadata, not used as the author, so the author set stays conventional).
If you are a dispatched **subagent**, add `--agent <your-name>` (e.g. `--agent
blueprint-reviewer`) to record your identity; it is stored in metadata and shown
next to the role in the UI, keeping the author itself a clean role. Items and
comments are also auto-tagged with the run/session provenance (a small chip in the
UI); you do not pass this yourself.

New inbox items use a two-part body:

- First paragraph = concise title. Keep it short enough to scan in one UI row
  (aim for under ~100 characters); do not put the full explanation here.
- Second paragraph and later = non-empty description. Put the evidence, impact,
  next action, and relevant ids/files here. The description is **mandatory** — an
  item that is only a title is rejected.

Write bodies and comments in **Markdown**: the dashboard renders it. Use short
paragraphs and `-` bullet lists instead of one wall-of-text paragraph, `**bold**`
for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, files, and code
(`` `lem:foo` ``, `` `Foo.lean:42` ``). A long unstructured blob is hard to scan.

Do not create an item whose body is only a title. If you need a `[persistent]` or
`[temporary]` tag, put it at the start of the title paragraph and keep the rest
of that paragraph concise.

Before opening a new item, search the open inbox for the same topic using
`horizon inbox list --status open --query "key words" --json` and, when relevant,
`--project P` / `--to R`. If a live item already covers the same issue, do not
open a duplicate: add a comment with the new evidence, or edit the body if the
main description is stale. If you discover an open item is obsolete, already
solved, or superseded, add a short closing comment explaining why and then run
`horizon inbox complete <id>`.

- `horizon inbox comment <id> --body "..."` — progress note on an item (record
  what you did; not to ask the human for clarification). Do not write your name
  into the comment body; the author is set automatically. Drop a comment at each **key advance**
  while you work an item (a milestone reached, a route ruled out, a blocker hit),
  not only at the end — these comments are the item's visible progress trail.
- **Write comments as mathematics, for a human.** State the key step that
  succeeded in human-readable mathematical language (LaTeX for formulas), naming
  the result and the idea — e.g. "Proved $H^i(X,\mathcal{F})=0$ for $i>\dim X$ by
  reducing to the affine case via Čech cohomology." Mention Lean declaration
  names and file paths only as supporting detail; the substance is the maths, not

input.description: Read horizon-commit skill

data.content:
---
name: horizon-commit
description: Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
---

Commit your work yourself, semantically, as you finish coherent pieces — don't
leave one giant undifferentiated snapshot for the system to sweep up. Good
commits are how the dashboard's change view and a human reviewer understand what
each session actually did.

## How

Use the Horizon CLI (never raw `git` on the ledger — the workspace repo is
out-of-tree with excludes, a secret guard, and provenance trailers that a bare
`git` would bypass):

- `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<message>" <files…>` — commit the files you changed.
  Paths are relative to your current directory (your project dir) or absolute.
- `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<message>" --changed` — commit everything you
  changed in the project (excludes the shared Horizon state, which the system
  commits separately).

Provenance (run / session / role / task / projects) is stamped automatically —
do **not** pass it. The commit is attributed to your role.

## Messages — math first

Write the message the way you'd tell a mathematician what you did: the result
and the idea, in human-readable prose with LaTeX where it helps; mention Lean
declaration names / files only as supporting detail. Mirror the house style,
e.g. `Prove H^i(X,F)=0 for i>dim X via the Čech-to-derived comparison`. One
coherent step per commit; split unrelated work into separate commits.

## Concurrency — you are not alone

Other runs may be committing to the **same** ledger at the same time. That's
fine and intended (it helps everyone converge faster):

- Commits are a single shared branch — they serialise and never conflict as a
  git *merge*; if `horizon commit` hits contention it already retried, so trust
  its result.
- Commit only the files **you** changed. On a shared worktree your commit can
  still capture another run's in-flight edit to the same file — that's expected
  and acceptable; the message + trailers still record your intent.
- Never `reset`, `rebase`, force-push, or otherwise rewrite the ledger. `horizon
  commit` only ever appends a commit.

## When

Commit at each coherent milestone (a lemma closed, a file completed), not only
at the end. Anything you don't commit is still captured by the system's
end-of-session sweep, but as an unlabelled blob — so commit it yourself to keep
the history legible.

`restrictFunctor j` is `SheafOfModules.pushforward φ` for the canonical ring-sheaf
morphism `φ` induced by `j` (see `restrictFunctor_eq_pushforward`). Because the
base sites are the *small* categories `Opens U`, `Opens X`, the presheaf-level

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace SheafOfModules

variable {U X : Scheme.{u}} (j : U ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion j]

/-- The canonical morphism of sheaves of rings underlying restriction along an open
immersion `j : U ⟶ X`. This is exactly the datum Mathlib keeps inline inside
`Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor`, exposed here as a named definition so that the
identification `restrictFunctor j = SheafOfModules.pushforward _` (and hence the
extension-by-zero adjunction) can be stated. Project-local because Mathlib does not
name it. -/
noncomputable def restrictionRingSheafHom :
    U.ringCatSheaf ⟶ (j.opensFunctor.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u}
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)).obj X.ringCatSheaf :=
  ⟨Functor.whiskerRight ({ app V := (j.appIso V.unop).inv } :
    U.presheaf ⟶ j.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf) (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩

/-- `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor j` is, definitionally, the sheaf-of-modules
pushforward along the continuous open-immersion site map with the restriction
ring-sheaf datum. This exposes the defeq that powers `extensionByZeroAdjunction`. -/
lemma restrictFunctor_eq_pushforward :
    Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor j
      = SheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (restrictionRingSheafHom j) :=
  rfl

/-- **Extension by zero** `j_!` for an open immersion `j : U ⟶ X` of schemes: the
left adjoint of the restriction functor `j^* = restrictFunctor j`. Realized as the
sheaf-of-modules pullback `SheafOfModules.pullback` along the restriction ring-sheaf
datum `restrictionRingSheafHom j`, whose existence as a left adjoint is supplied by
Mathlib's `PullbackContinuous` (small `Opens` sites ⟹ the presheaf-level left
adjoint exists ⟹ it sheafifies). Project-local: this left adjoint is **absent** from
Mathlib v4.30.0. -/
noncomputable def extensionByZero : U.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pullback.{u} (restrictionRingSheafHom j)

variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C]

/-- `FullyFaithful.homEquiv` as a natural isomorphism. -/
@[simps! hom_app inv_app]
def homNatIso {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D] {F : C ⥤ D} (hF : F.FullyFaithful) (X : C) :
    F.op ⋙ uliftYoneda.{v₁}.obj (F.obj X) ≅ uliftYoneda.{v₂}.obj X :=
  NatIso.ofComponents
    (fun Y => Equiv.toIso (Equiv.ulift.trans <| hF.homEquiv.symm.trans Equiv.ulift.symm))
    (fun f => by ext; exact Equiv.ulift.injective (hF.map_injective (by simp)))

/-- `FullyFaithful.homEquiv` as a natural isomorphism. -/
@[deprecated homNatIso (since := "2025-10-28")]
def homNatIsoMaxRight {D : Type u₂} [Category.{max v₁ v₂} D] {F : C ⥤ D} (hF : F.FullyFaithful)
    (X : C) : F.op ⋙ yoneda.obj (F.obj X) ≅ uliftYoneda.{v₂}.obj X :=
  isoWhiskerLeft F.op (uliftYonedaIsoYoneda.symm.app _) ≪≫ hF.homNatIso _ ≪≫
    NatIso.ofComponents (fun _ => Equiv.toIso (Equiv.ulift.trans Equiv.ulift.symm))

/-- `FullyFaithful.homEquiv` as a natural isomorphism. -/
@[simps! +dsimpLhs]
def compUliftYonedaCompWhiskeringLeft {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D] {F : C ⥤ D}
    (hF : F.FullyFaithful) :
    F ⋙ uliftYoneda.{v₁} ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj F.op ≅ uliftYoneda.{v₂} :=
  NatIso.ofComponents (fun X => hF.homNatIso _) fun f => by
    ext; exact Equiv.ulift.injective (hF.map_injective (by simp))

@[deprecated (since := "2025-10-20")] alias compYonedaCompWhiskeringLeft :=
  compUliftYonedaCompWhiskeringLeft

/-- `FullyFaithful.homEquiv` as a natural isomorphism. -/
@[deprecated compUliftYonedaCompWhiskeringLeft (since := "2025-10-28")]
def compYonedaCompWhiskeringLeftMaxRight {D : Type u₂} [Category.{max v₁ v₂} D] {F : C ⥤ D}
    (hF : F.FullyFaithful) : F ⋙ yoneda ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj F.op ≅ uliftYoneda.{v₂} := by

    F ⋙ uliftCoyoneda.{v₁}.obj (op (F.obj X)) ≅ uliftCoyoneda.{v₂}.obj (op X) :=

    (fun f => by ext; exact Equiv.ulift.injective (hF.map_injective (by simp)))

/-- `FullyFaithful.homEquiv` as a natural isomorphism, using coyoneda. -/
@[simps! +dsimpLhs]
def compUliftCoyonedaCompWhiskeringLeft {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D] {F : C ⥤ D}
    (hF : F.FullyFaithful) :

lemma isIso_app (V : Y.Opens) (hV : V ≤ f.opensRange) : IsIso (f.app V) := by
  rw [show V = f ''ᵁ f ⁻¹ᵁ V from Opens.ext (Set.image_preimage_eq_of_subset hV).symm]

/-- The isomorphism `Γ(Y, f(U)) ≅ Γ(X, U)` induced by an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y`. -/
def appIso (U) : Γ(Y, f ''ᵁ U) ≅ Γ(X, U) :=
  (asIso <| LocallyRingedSpace.IsOpenImmersion.invApp f.toLRSHom U).symm

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
theorem appIso_inv_naturality {U V : X.Opens} (i : op U ⟶ op V) :
    X.presheaf.map i ≫ (f.appIso V).inv =
      (f.appIso U).inv ≫ Y.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.op.map i) :=
  PresheafedSpace.IsOpenImmersion.inv_naturality _ _

theorem appIso_hom (U) :
    (f.appIso U).hom = f.app (f ''ᵁ U) ≫ X.presheaf.map
